message:
fix: abort mdn-data JSON inlining when next lines don't match expected pattern

Previously, when nextLine1 or nextLine2 didn't match the expected
mdn-data require() patterns, the code still pushed the inlined JSON but
didn't properly skip the mismatched lines — producing output with both
inlined JSON AND the original un-skipped lines, corrupting the file.

Now, when either line doesn't match, the original line is pushed
unchanged and JSON inlining is aborted entirely, letting the downstream
residual-require check catch the problem safely.

Also narrows the post-patch require() check from the overly broad
`require(` (which matches comments and strings) to the specific
`require('mdn-data` to avoid false positives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
